task: Write a bash script that backs up a PostgreSQL database to S3 with rotation, logging, and error notifications

criteria.description: Evaluates proper configuration of pg_dump for production-grade consistent snapshots, including serializable isolation, connection security, and PostgreSQL-specific backup parameters that ensure data integrity under concurrent workloads
criteria.pass_condition: Must use --serializable-deferrable flag, implement secure credential handling (environment variables or .pgpass reference), and include minimum 3 of these pg_dump optimizations: --verbose, --compress=9, --format=custom, --no-privileges, --no-owner

sub_attributes.description: Implementation of --serializable-deferrable for consistent snapshots without blocking writers
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if --serializable-deferrable used with proper retry logic for deferrable transaction waits, 0.7 if --serializable-deferrable present but no retry handling, 0.4 if uses --single-transaction without serializable, 0.0 if no consistency controls

sub_attributes.description: Secure connection handling with SSL enforcement and credential management
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if uses --ssl-mode=require with .pgpass at 600 permissions and --no-password flag, 0.7 if 2 of 3 security measures present, 0.4 if only SSL or credential security implemented, 0.0 if plaintext connections or hardcoded credentials

sub_attributes.description: PostgreSQL-specific dump optimizations for production environments
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if includes 4+ optimizations (--verbose, --compress=9, --format=custom, --no-privileges, --no-owner), 0.7 if 3 optimizations present, 0.4 if 1-2 basic optimizations, 0.0 if plain pg_dump with no optimization flags

fail_examples: pg_dump with --single-transaction but missing --serializable-deferrable, or SSL connection without .pgpass security
criteria.research_basis: [Partial] SOC 2 Compliance: PostgreSQL can support SOC 2 compliance by implementing security controls, access control mechanisms, data encryption, and auditing capabilities

criteria.description: Implements comprehensive PostgreSQL-aware error detection and recovery mechanisms for all failure modes including database-specific errors, network issues, storage problems, and partial failures with appropriate retry logic and escalation patterns
criteria.pass_condition: Must implement exponential backoff retry for both PostgreSQL-specific errors (connection failures, serializable transaction conflicts with error codes 40001, 40P01) and general transient failures, handle minimum 4 failure scenarios including disk space and connection timeouts with specific exit codes, include error classification for at least 3 PostgreSQL-specific scenarios, and provide proper exit codes for monitoring integration

sub_attributes.description: Proper retry handling for PostgreSQL serializable transaction deferrals and conflicts
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if implements exponential backoff specifically for serialization failures with max retry limits, 0.7 if basic retry for serializable deferrals, 0.4 if generic retry without PostgreSQL error code awareness, 0.0 if no retry logic for serializable conflicts

sub_attributes.description: Distinguishes PostgreSQL-specific error conditions from generic failures
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if categorizes errors by PostgreSQL error codes with different handling, 0.7 if distinguishes 2-3 PostgreSQL error types, 0.4 if basic PostgreSQL vs system error separation, 0.0 if treats all errors generically

sub_attributes.description: Handles common PostgreSQL operational failure scenarios with appropriate responses
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if handles 4+ scenarios (connection timeout, disk space, lock conflicts, permission issues, SSL failures), 0.7 if covers 3 operational scenarios, 0.4 if handles 1-2 basic error cases, 0.0 if minimal error handling

pass_examples: Error handling that retries with exponential backoff when pg_dump returns serialization error 40001, but immediately fails and alerts on authentication or disk space errors
fail_examples: Generic bash error handling that retries any pg_dump failure without understanding PostgreSQL-specific error conditions
criteria.research_basis: [Partial] Error Handling and Retry Logic: Exponential backoff with jitter, circuit breakers, partial failure recovery

criteria.description: Implements secure AWS CLI integration using IAM roles instead of hardcoded credentials, with proper S3 encryption and multipart upload handling for large databases
criteria.pass_condition: Must use IAM role authentication (no hardcoded credentials), implement S3 server-side encryption (SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS), and include aws s3 cp with --storage-class parameter
pass_examples: aws s3 cp backup.sql.gz s3://bucket/backup/ --sse aws:kms --storage-class STANDARD_IA using IAM role authentication
fail_examples: Script contains AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY variables, or uploads without encryption
criteria.research_basis: Great: Uses IAM roles with temporary tokens, .pgpass with restrictive permissions, --ssl-mode=require

criteria.description: Implements intelligent backup rotation using S3 lifecycle policies or script-based retention with multiple tiers and cost optimization through storage class transitions
criteria.pass_condition: Must implement either S3 lifecycle policies with object tagging OR script-based rotation with minimum 3 retention tiers (daily/weekly/monthly), and include storage class transitions for cost optimization

sub_attributes.description: Implements comprehensive backup rotation with multiple retention periods
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if uses S3 lifecycle policies with proper tagging or implements 3+ retention tiers, 0.7 if script-based rotation with 2 retention periods, 0.4 if basic retention logic, 0.0 if simple file deletion without tiering

sub_attributes.description: Utilizes S3 storage classes for cost optimization across backup lifecycle
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if implements Standard → IA → Glacier transitions with appropriate timing, 0.7 if uses 2 storage classes with transitions, 0.4 if single storage class with cost consideration, 0.0 if no storage class optimization

sub_attributes.description: Addresses compliance requirements in retention policy design
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if implements compliance-aware retention with legal hold capabilities, 0.7 if mentions compliance requirements in retention logic, 0.4 if basic long-term retention, 0.0 if no compliance consideration

pass_examples: S3 lifecycle policy that moves backups to IA after 30 days, Glacier after 90 days, with daily/weekly/monthly retention tiers
fail_examples: Simple script that deletes backups older than X days without tiered retention or cost optimization
criteria.research_basis: Automated lifecycle rules matching retention requirements, graduated storage classes, automated cleanup

criteria.description: Implements backup integrity verification through validation checks, checksum verification, and PostgreSQL-specific integrity checks to ensure backup quality
criteria.pass_condition: Must include pg_restore --list validation to verify backup structure, implement backup size and checksum verification against expected ranges, and include at least 2 integrity checks (file corruption detection, metadata validation)
pass_examples: Script performs pg_restore --list validation followed by restoration to test database with row count verification
fail_examples: Backup script only checks if pg_dump command returns success code without any restoration or integrity testing
criteria.research_basis: A backup that cannot be restored is not a backup

criteria.description: Implements structured logging compatible with centralized monitoring systems and integrates with incident management tools for comprehensive backup monitoring
criteria.pass_condition: Must output structured logs (JSON format recommended), implement minimum 3 alert severity levels, and include backup metrics (duration, size, success rate)

sub_attributes.description: Outputs machine-readable logs compatible with centralized logging systems
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if uses JSON structured logging with proper field standardization, 0.7 if implements key-value structured format, 0.4 if consistent log format but not structured, 0.0 if unstructured logging

sub_attributes.description: Integrates with incident management and notification systems
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if implements webhook/API integration with PagerDuty/Slack with severity levels, 0.7 if email alerts with proper severity classification, 0.4 if basic notification system, 0.0 if no alerting integration

sub_attributes.description: Collects and reports comprehensive backup operation metrics
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if tracks 4+ metrics (duration, size, success rate, error types) with timestamps, 0.7 if tracks 3 key metrics, 0.4 if basic success/failure tracking, 0.0 if no metrics collection

pass_examples: JSON logs with timestamp, backup_duration, backup_size_bytes, and Slack webhook integration with ERROR/WARN/INFO severity levels
fail_examples: Simple echo statements to stdout without structure or external notification integration
criteria.research_basis: Dashboard with core metrics, structured logs for analysis, integration with incident response systems

criteria.description: Implements comprehensive encryption strategy adhering to SOC 2/PCI DSS requirements with proper key management and audit logging
criteria.pass_condition: Must implement encryption in transit (SSL) and at rest (S3 SSE), include audit logging for backup operations, and demonstrate 3-2-1 backup rule compliance
pass_examples: SSL PostgreSQL connection with KMS-encrypted S3 storage and comprehensive audit logging
fail_examples: Plaintext database connection or unencrypted S3 storage without audit trail
criteria.research_basis: TDE or equivalent storage encryption, AES-256 encryption, HSM or cloud KMS key management with regular rotation

criteria.description: Evaluates implementation of advanced backup features using pg_dump parameters and bash scripting techniques
criteria.pass_condition: Implements at least 2 of: parallel pg_dump operations using --jobs parameter, custom compression implementation, backup splitting for large databases, or advanced pg_dump options (--exclude-table-data, --section)

sub_attributes.description: Configures parallel backup and restore operations
sub_attributes.measurement: binary_present

sub_attributes.description: Supports full/differential/incremental backup types
sub_attributes.measurement: count_types

sub_attributes.description: Implements ZSTD compression and encryption
sub_attributes.measurement: binary_both

sub_attributes.description: Supports delta restore capability
sub_attributes.measurement: binary_present

pass_examples: pgBackRest configuration with process-max=4, backup-type=incr, compress-type=zst, cipher-type=aes-256-cbc
fail_examples: Basic pg_dump without compression or parallel operations
criteria.research_basis: Modern PostgreSQL backup solutions should support parallel backup and restore, full/differential/incremental backups, delta restore, ZSTD compression, and encryption

criteria.description: Implements WAL-related backup considerations and provides framework for Point-in-Time Recovery integration
criteria.pass_condition: Includes WAL backup functionality using pg_receivewal or archive directory copying, demonstrates checkpoint coordination with pg_start_backup/pg_stop_backup functions, and provides documented procedures for PITR setup

sub_attributes.description: Sets archive_mode=on in PostgreSQL configuration
sub_attributes.measurement: binary_present

sub_attributes.description: Implements proper archive_command for WAL shipping
sub_attributes.measurement: functional_test

sub_attributes.description: Stores WAL files in durable location (S3/equivalent)
sub_attributes.measurement: location_check

sub_attributes.description: Documents and tests Point-in-Time Recovery procedure
sub_attributes.measurement: documentation_present

pass_examples: archive_command = 'aws s3 cp %p s3://bucket/wal/%f' with archive_mode = on and recovery testing
fail_examples: Only pg_dump backups without WAL archiving or PITR capability
criteria.research_basis: Professional implementations include archive_mode=on with archive_command configured for Point-in-Time Recovery capability

criteria.description: Provides comprehensive disaster recovery support through documented procedures and recovery validation
criteria.pass_condition: Includes executable recovery verification commands, documents specific RPO/RTO implications of the backup strategy, provides step-by-step recovery procedures as executable comments, and includes backup validation routines that can verify recoverability

sub_attributes.description: Clearly defines Recovery Point Objective and Recovery Time Objective
sub_attributes.measurement: specific_values

sub_attributes.description: Implements automated recovery procedures and scripts
sub_attributes.measurement: script_present

sub_attributes.description: Establishes regular disaster recovery testing schedule
sub_attributes.measurement: schedule_documented

sub_attributes.description: Maintains comprehensive disaster recovery documentation
sub_attributes.measurement: completeness_check

pass_examples: RPO: 15 minutes, RTO: 4 hours, automated restore scripts, quarterly DR tests with documented results
fail_examples: No defined recovery objectives, manual-only procedures, no testing schedule
criteria.research_basis: Clear RPO/RTO requirements, documented disaster recovery plans, automated recovery procedures, regular testing
